steps.title: Find the base angle
steps.content: Since $AC=CD$, triangle $ACD$ is isosceles. The two base angles are equal, so each base angle is $$\frac{180^\circ-104^\circ}{2}=38^\circ$$

steps.title: Transfer the angle
steps.content: From the figure, $D$, $C$, and $B$ are collinear, and $D$, $E$, and $A$ are collinear. Therefore the angle at $D$ in triangle $BDE$ is $38^\circ$.

steps.title: Use the exterior angle
steps.content: In triangle $BDE$, the exterior angle at $E$ equals the sum of the two remote interior angles:$$x=45^\circ+38^\circ=83^\circ$$The answer is 83.
